You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

[4-6] The scene cuts to a wide shot of a woman with long dark hair in a purple top standing behind a kitchen counter. She gestures with open hands near a white bowl and whisk. Ingredients and the finished graveyard cake are arranged on the surface. Behind her, white cabinets are decorated with Halloween paper pumpkins and bats.

[6-8] The woman in the dark purple top continues gesturing with her hands near a white bowl. An orange lower-third graphic appears in the bottom left corner featuring the text "LIMOR SUSS" in black capital letters. [4-10] The scene cuts to a woman in a purple top standing in a kitchen decorated with paper pumpkins and bats. She gestures near a white bowl and whisk. An orange lower-third graphic displays "LIMOR SUSS" and "LIMORSUSS.COM". [10-14] After a brief close-up of the woman, the view shifts to a hand placing a white marshmallow ghost with black eyes onto the chocolate frosting, amidst orange and purple sprinkles and green gummy candies. From 16s to 20s, the camera focuses on a rectangular Halloween graveyard cake on a white board, surrounded by black spiderweb placemats. The dessert features a perimeter fence of vertical chocolate wafers enclosing dark frosting topped with orange and purple sprinkles. Inside the fence stand white chocolate tombstones inscribed with "BOO" in black icing, white marshmallow ghosts with black eyes, green gummy candies, and black licorice trees. Pink crumbs line the base. From 20s to 24s, the scene cuts

[54-56] A close-up captures a hand using a black offset spatula to smooth dark chocolate frosting along the side of a rectangular cake covered in orange and purple sprinkles. [56-60] The camera cuts to a woman in a dark purple top standing behind a kitchen counter adorned with Halloween decorations, including "BOO" signs and pumpkins. She gestures with open hands before pointing both index fingers upward. The counter displays black cauldrons filled with candy, the rectangular cake, and a chocolate graveyard cake. [60-62] The shot returns to a close-up of hands holding a dark chocolate bar above the cake. Behind the cake, a white pedestal plate displays stacks of chocolate bars next to a small glass bowl filled with wrapped candies.

[62-70] Hands decorate a rectangular chocolate cake topped with orange and purple sprinkles by pressing vertical dark chocolate wafer bars into the frosting along the edge. Initially, a single bar is positioned, followed by a second adjacent bar, beginning a perimeter fence. The process continues as more bars are added to the row, creating a barrier around the dessert on a white board. In the background, a white pedestal plate holds stacks of similar chocolate bars next to a glass bowl filled with wrapped candies.

[70-78] Hands press vertical chocolate wafer bars into the frosting along the side and corners of a rectangular cake topped with orange and purple sprinkles. The action extends the fence perimeter as more bars are added to the row. By the end, the chocolate bars surround the entire dessert on the white board, creating a complete fence. Behind the cake, a white pedestal displays stacks of chocolate bars beside a glass bowl filled with wrapped candies.

[52-54] A woman in a dark purple top cuts a rectangular chocolate cake topped with sprinkles on a white board, positioned between black cauldrons filled with candy and a finished graveyard dessert. [78-80] In a wide shot, she holds a white chocolate bar, gesturing near the rectangular cake featuring a chocolate fence and the completed graveyard cake. [80-82] Close-up hands break the white chocolate bar into segments over a white plate holding two pieces, next to the cake surrounded by vertical dark chocolate wafers. [82-84] Hands pipe "BO" in dark icing onto a white chocolate piece while a finger points, with the wafer-fenced cake visible to the right.

recent_thinks: [14-16] The camera reveals ingredients on the counter, featuring three black cauldrons filled with wrapped candies including Hershey's bars and red foil chocolates. A package of green and red Twizzlers lies in the foreground on a black spiderweb placemat. To the right, the decorated graveyard cake sits on a white board, while a stainless steel stove is visible in the background.

[24-26] The woman in the purple top lifts a white bowl and pours a dark brown mixture into a rectangular metal baking pan. She holds a whisk in her right hand to assist the flow while smiling as the batter fills the pan. A jar of frosting sits nearby on the counter.

[84-86] A hand pipes black icing onto a white chocolate rectangle on a plate, forming the word "BOO". A finger steadies the piece during the process. In the background, the rectangular cake surrounded by vertical chocolate wafers sits on the counter.

[86-88] The view cuts to a wide shot of a woman in a dark purple top gesturing with open hands behind a counter. In front of her are a rectangular cake with a chocolate fence and a graveyard cake. Black cauldrons filled with candy sit to the left, and Halloween decorations hang on the white tiled wall.

[88-90] The woman continues to speak, moving her hands slightly while standing behind the counter. The chocolate-fenced cake and graveyard dessert remain in front of her. Black cauldrons filled with candy are visible on the left, and Halloween decorations like pumpkins hang on the white tiled wall.

[90-92] A hand places white chocolate rectangles into the chocolate wafer fence surrounding the cake. One tombstone displays "BOO" in black icing, while another features black wavy lines. Orange and purple sprinkles cover the cake surface.

samples.output: <think>A hand places a white chocolate rectangle with black wavy lines into the frosting within the chocolate wafer fence. Nearby, a white chocolate piece reading "BOO" and another with wavy lines stand upright as tombstones on the cake covered in orange and purple sprinkles.</think><action>silent</action>

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

compressed_segments.text: [0-4] A rectangular Halloween cake sits on a counter, enclosed by a chocolate wafer fence with white marshmallow ghosts and tombstones reading "BOO" inside. Pink sprinkles surround the base. Text overlays appear: "HOW TO MAKE" at the top left, followed by "A 'GHOULISH GRAVEYARD' DESSERT" at the bottom. [4-10] The scene cuts to a woman in a purple top standing in a kitchen decorated with paper pumpkins and bats. She gestures near a white bowl and whisk. An orange lower-third graphic displays "LIMOR SUSS" and "LIMORSUSS.COM". [10-14] After a brief close-up of the woman, the view shifts to a hand placing a white marshmallow ghost with black eyes onto the chocolate frosting, amidst orange and purple sprinkles and green gummy candies. From 16s to 20s, the camera focuses on a rectangular Halloween graveyard cake on a white board, surrounded by black spiderweb placemats. The dessert features a perimeter fence of vertical chocolate wafers enclosing dark frosting topped with orange and purple sprinkles. Inside the fence stand white chocolate tombstones inscribed with "BOO" in black icing, white marshmallow ghosts with black eyes, green gummy candies, and black licorice trees. Pink crumbs line the base. From 20s to 24s, the scene cuts

[86-90] A woman in a dark purple top gestures behind a counter featuring a rectangular cake with a chocolate fence and a separate graveyard cake. Black cauldrons filled with candy sit to the left, and Halloween decorations hang on the white tiled wall. [90-94] A hand places white chocolate rectangles into the frosting within the chocolate wafer fence. One tombstone displays "BOO" in black icing, while others feature black wavy lines. Orange and purple sprinkles cover the cake surface. The hand positions the wavy-lined chocolate pieces upright next to the "BOO" marker on the sprinkle-covered cake.

From 94s to 100s, a hand places a white chocolate rectangle inscribed with "BOO" in black icing into the chocolate frosting of a rectangular cake. The cake is covered in orange and purple sprinkles and surrounded by a fence of vertical chocolate wafers. Other white chocolate tombstones featuring black wavy lines or text stand upright nearby. At 100s, a woman with long dark hair wearing a purple top holds up a twisted black licorice stick. The scene cuts to a close-up where a hand uses silver scissors to cut the black licorice strip into smaller segments over a white rectangular plate.

[102-106] A hand holds a long, twisted black licorice stick over a white rectangular plate while silver scissors cut through the candy, snipping it into smaller segments. [106-110] The hand then places the cut black licorice sticks upright into the chocolate frosting of a rectangular cake. The cake is decorated with orange and purple sprinkles and white chocolate tombstones inscribed with "BOO" and wavy lines. The licorice cluster is positioned near the back left corner next to a tombstone, forming a tree-like decoration.

[110-118] A hand inserts twisted black licorice sticks vertically into the front left corner of a chocolate cake, creating a cluster of spooky trees. The dessert is designed as a graveyard, featuring a chocolate wafer fence around the perimeter and chocolate frosting covered in orange and purple sprinkles. White chocolate squares act as tombstones, some inscribed with "BOO" and others with wavy lines. The new licorice placement joins existing clusters in the back corners, completing the eerie landscape.

recent_thinks: [14-16] The camera reveals ingredients on the counter, featuring three black cauldrons filled with wrapped candies including Hershey's bars and red foil chocolates. A package of green and red Twizzlers lies in the foreground on a black spiderweb placemat. To the right, the decorated graveyard cake sits on a white board, while a stainless steel stove is visible in the background.

[24-26] The woman in the purple top lifts a white bowl and pours a dark brown mixture into a rectangular metal baking pan. She holds a whisk in her right hand to assist the flow while smiling as the batter fills the pan. A jar of frosting sits nearby on the counter.

[84-86] A hand pipes black icing onto a white chocolate rectangle on a plate, forming the word "BOO". A finger steadies the piece during the process. In the background, the rectangular cake surrounded by vertical chocolate wafers sits on the counter.

[118-120] A hand adjusts a cluster of twisted black licorice sticks inserted into the front left corner of the chocolate cake. The black candy strands stand upright next to a white chocolate tombstone featuring black wavy lines, surrounded by orange and purple sprinkles inside the chocolate wafer fence.

[120-122] A hand adjusts a cluster of twisted black licorice sticks inserted into the front left corner of the chocolate cake. The licorice stands next to a white chocolate tombstone inscribed with "BOO". After positioning the candy trees, the hand withdraws from the frame, leaving the graveyard decoration complete.

samples.output: <think>A hand withdraws from the frame after positioning a cluster of black licorice sticks upright in the front left corner of the chocolate cake. The candy strands stand next to a white chocolate tombstone inscribed with "BOO", surrounded by orange and purple sprinkles within the chocolate wafer fence.</think><action>silent</action>

compressed_segments.text: [0-4] A rectangular Halloween cake sits on a counter, enclosed by a chocolate wafer fence with white marshmallow ghosts and tombstones reading "BOO" inside. [4-10] The scene cuts to a woman in a purple top standing in a kitchen decorated with paper pumpkins and bats. She gestures near a white bowl and whisk. From 16s to 20s, the camera focuses on a rectangular Halloween graveyard cake on a white board, surrounded by black spiderweb placemats. The dessert features a perimeter fence of vertical chocolate wafers enclosing dark frosting topped with orange and purple sprinkles. Inside the fence stand white chocolate tombstones inscribed with "BOO" in black icing, white marshmallow ghosts with black eyes, green gummy candies, and black licorice trees. Pink crumbs line the base. From 20s to 24s, the scene cuts

[70-78] Hands press vertical chocolate wafer bars into the frosting along the side and corners of a rectangular cake topped with orange and purple sprinkles. The action extends the fence perimeter as more bars are added to the row. By the end, the chocolate bars surround the entire dessert on the white board, creating a complete fence. Behind the cake, a white pedestal displays stacks of chocolate bars beside a glass bowl filled with wrapped candies.

[52-54] A woman in a dark purple top cuts a rectangular chocolate cake topped with sprinkles on a white board, positioned between black cauldrons filled with candy and a finished graveyard dessert. [80-82] Close-up hands break the white chocolate bar into segments over a white plate holding two pieces, next to the cake surrounded by vertical dark chocolate wafers. [82-84] Hands pipe "BO" in dark icing onto a white chocolate piece while a finger points, with the wafer-fenced cake visible to the right.

[86-90] A woman in a dark purple top gestures behind a counter featuring a rectangular cake with a chocolate fence and a separate graveyard cake. Black cauldrons filled with candy sit to the left, and Halloween decorations hang on the white tiled wall. [90-94] A hand places white chocolate rectangles into the frosting within the chocolate wafer fence. One tombstone displays "BOO" in black icing, while others feature black wavy lines. Orange and purple sprinkles cover the cake surface. The hand positions the wavy-lined chocolate pieces upright next to the "BOO" marker on the sprinkle-covered cake.

From 94s to 100s, a hand places a white chocolate rectangle inscribed with "BOO" in black icing into the chocolate frosting of a rectangular cake. The cake is covered in orange and purple sprinkles and surrounded by a fence of vertical chocolate wafers. Other white chocolate tombstones featuring black wavy lines or text stand upright nearby. At 100s, a woman with long dark hair wearing a purple top holds up a twisted black licorice stick. The scene cuts to a close-up where a hand uses silver scissors to cut the black licorice strip into smaller segments over a white rectangular plate.

[24-26] The woman in the purple top lifts a white bowl and pours a dark brown mixture into a rectangular metal baking pan. She holds a whisk in her right hand to assist the flow while smiling as the batter fills the pan. A jar of frosting sits nearby on the counter.

input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).
